ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Mediation
Off Calendar Nov-26-2024 Continued To Jan-14-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Christopher Neary for Joseph Capko, Proposed Administrator (via Zoom).
Emilie Calhoun for Nancy Nowotny, Proposed Executor.
Nancy Nowotny is present via Zoom.

Discussions were held with regards to procedural issues and concerns from counsel regarding alleged misrepresentations. Mr. Neary asserted that the remaining procedural issues could be resolved with a supplemental filing and requested a continuance to amend the petition and address the outstanding matters, citing a dispute over the scope of assets in the estate.

Ms. Calhoun requested dismissal of the probate petition, arguing there are no significant assets requiring administration. She raised concerns about alleged gamesmanship and delays, including issues form a prior mediation that was not successful.

The court declined to dismiss the petition but ordered the parties to meet and confer no later than December 24, 2024, to resolve outstanding issues, including the scope of the settlement agreement and release. The parties were ordered to file a joint report by January 7, 2025, summarizing the results of the meet and confer efforts. The court continues the matter to January 14, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Court reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto, CSR #5229, peggytsujimoto@gmail.com, 415-828-1919 (D204)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Jul-30-2024 Continued To Oct-08-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christopher Neary for Joseph Capko.
Emilie Calhoun for and with Nancy Nowotny (objector) by zoom.

Mrs. Calhoun noted that Mr. Neary's petition has been continued multiple times for failure to comply with the probate examiner's notes. Mr. Neary stated that complying with rule 418 is difficult to do because all of the heirs are located out of State, that he received a family chart 10-days ago, and that the only thing left to do is to obtain dates of death for the siblings and death certificate. Mrs. Calhoun stated that her client filed the family tree in the trust matter, that Mr. Neary has had it since December of 2022/more than one year, that Mr. Capko helped Mrs. Nowotny clean out the property, that Mr. Capko did not ask for any records or any books at the time, and that all of the items in the property were moldy and in horrible condition. Mrs. Calhoun also provided history of the case including the trust matter and stated that she would like the court to dismiss Mr. Neary's petition because there is nothing to probate. Further discussion was held regarding Mr. Capko's position that Mrs. Nowotny distributed assets that were bequeathed to him to other parties who were disinherited, the outstanding notes in Mr. Neary's petition dating back to March 26, 2023, Mrs. Calhoun's petition needing to amended if she is asking for general appointment of her client, the assignments of the records and the books being assets of the trust, Mrs. Calhoun's position that Mr. Capko is only entitled to books and records that were purchased after 2006, Mr. Neary initially stating the value of the records and the books at $35,000.00 and now valuing them at $70,000.00, etc. Mrs. Calhoun offered 4 or 5 books to Mr. Neary in open court. Mr. Neary refused to accept them. The titles of the books were read into the record. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than October 1, 2024. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by October 4, 2024. The Petition for Probate of Will and Letters Testamentary, and the Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed are continued to and the Parties are to return on October 8, 2024, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. Mr. Neary is to substantially address the examiner's notes by October 4th, or his petition will be taken off calendar on October 8th. The parties are to obtain a copy of the mediation order from the court's website.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Reporter: Elizabeth J. Lee, CSR #5828, 415-753-3474, lizleecsr@att.net

Off Calendar May-16-2023 Continued To Jul-11-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
The following matters were heard: PTR-21-304766 The R. Gene Geisler Trust and PES-23-306090 Estate of R. Gene Geisler.

Appearances; Emilie Calhoun appearing in person for and with Nancy Nowotny (Petitioner).
Christopher Neary appearing in person for and with Joseph Capko and Judith Bailey.
Angela Pourtabib (Court Reporter) in person.

Discussion was held regarding the issue of whether or not there is a stipulation, whether a will has to be admitted, Mr. Neary not waving notice, Mrs. Calhoun's request to the Court to appoint her client as a special administrator, the parties agreeing on the need to get documents from Mr. Hughes, the value of the library, the parties being unable to agree on the Courts suggestion of having a third party appointed, etc. The Court stated that it is prepared to grant the 850 Petition as to the Mendocino Bank account (order signed in open court). The Court informed Mrs. Calhoun that there is no competing petition before it today. The remainder of the 850 Petition, and the Petition for letters of administration with will annexed are continued to July 11, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: Ateesh Datt. Deputy: Cristobal. Court Reporter; Angela Pourtabib (415)601-7054 apourtabib@gmail.com.
